page: Amazon Web Services r6a.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per physical core. It does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r6a.metal demonstrates top-tier performance. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores rank in the top 10%, reflecting the high processing density of the 192 vCPUs. Memory performance is also strong, with Passmark memory scores and small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 10%, while large-block read and write bandwidths rank in the top 25%. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, achieve top-tier results at 183.62 million operations per second. However, single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform in the average tier.

The bare-metal configuration offers dedicated hardware allocation, eliminating virtualization overhead and enabling direct access to CPU features like AMD SVM and cryptography extensions. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce latency compared to local NVMe options. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dense bare-metal architecture provide high resource density for memory-bound workloads, though the use of DDR4 memory represents an older generation compared to newer DDR5 standards.

This server type is configured for memory-intensive workloads that require massive parallel processing. Suitable applications include large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound scientific simulations. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM prompt processing, though the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale AI training or high-throughput text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores at 3.6 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the r6a.metal exhibits top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside outstanding Redis SET throughput of 183.62 million operations per second. Conversely, single-threaded operations and text processing fall into the average tier. The bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly resource-dense for memory-intensive applications. This configuration is best suited for large-memory databases, heavy software compilation, and parallel memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC 7R13 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.metal is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs and 1.5 TB of RAM for high-throughput, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores
Equipped with 1536 GB of DDR4 RAM providing 8 GB per core
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Achieves 183.62 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for in-memory databases, software compilation, and enterprise workloads
